answered.question: How should the parent_scope sentinel(s) work?
answered.answer: Reuse the existing UNSET_SCOPE / ALL_SCOPES pair for both scope and parent_scope. Kwarg name (scope: vs parent_scope:) disambiguates. Thread-local stack stores tuples; sentinel applies to whichever slot is being set.

answered.question: How should the resolver protocol shape look?
answered.answer: Tuple-returning resolver: Config.scope_resolver returns [scope, parent_scope]. Hard breaking change — every consumer with a custom scope_resolver must update on upgrade. Major version bump, explicit CHANGELOG entry, migration note in README. Carves out an exception to the milestone-level BC commitment for resolver shape only.

answered.question: How should Section and Field share the partition-tuple logic?
answered.answer: Inline duplicate the symmetric pattern in field/base.rb and section.rb (mirrors the existing for_entity pattern). Do NOT extract a Scopable concern in this phase. Defer extraction until a third caller appears or implementations diverge.

answered.question: How should `unscoped { }` interact with both keys?
answered.answer: Atomic bypass — unscoped { } drops both scope AND parent_scope predicates entirely. Multimap stays at the field_id level; no scope or parent_scope filtering inside unscoped. Plan-canonical. Preserves today's audit-query semantic.

answered.question: Display ordering — should the move helpers be backed by acts_as_list or an in-house module?
answered.answer: In-house partition-aware module on Field::Base and Section, keyed by (entity_type, scope, parent_scope). Wraps each move in with_lock for race safety, normalizes sort_order to consecutive integers (no gaps), and treats boundary moves as no-ops. Helper surface (move_higher / move_lower / move_to_top / move_to_bottom / insert_at) matches acts_as_list muscle memory but the implementation is local. Rationale: keeps the documented 'one hard dep, soft-detect everything else' codebase contract from PATTERNS.md; ~150 LoC of in-house code beats taking a runtime dep that every consumer would inherit.

answered.question: Default values — how to distinguish 'no value supplied' from 'explicit nil' in Value#value=?
answered.answer: UNSET_VALUE = Object.new.freeze as a private constant on TypedEAV::Value. typed_values.create(field: f) defaults to UNSET_VALUE → triggers field.default_value population. typed_values.create(field: f, value: nil) passes explicit nil → stores nil, no default re-application. Mirrors UNSET_SCOPE / ALL_SCOPES from PATTERNS.md §'Sentinel objects for distinguishing kwarg states.' Form paths (typed_values_attributes= and typed_eav_attributes=) always submit explicit values, so they bypass the sentinel — defaults only fire on the non-form construction path, matching the ROADMAP success criterion.

answered.question: Field#backfill_default! — execution model (batching, transactions, sync/async)?
answered.answer: find_each(batch_size: 1000) with transaction do … end per batch, synchronous by default. Skip rule is 'records with non-nil typed value for this field' (re-checked per batch, so reruns are idempotent). Per-batch transactions hold row locks only for the batch duration — safe on live tables with millions of rows. Recoverable on mid-run failure. No built-in Active Job dispatch; README documents the recipe for users who want async wrapping. Iteration scope respects (entity_type, scope, parent_scope) via the Phase 1 for_entity helper.

answered.question: Cascade behavior — where does field_dependent live, and how is the schema migration delivered?
answered.answer: Single in-phase migration. (1) Add field_dependent string column on typed_eav_fields, null: false, default: 'destroy'; allowed values 'destroy' / 'nullify' / 'restrict_with_error' (string for forward-compat, not enum). (2) Change typed_eav_values.field_id from NOT NULL to nullable. (3) Drop and recreate FK from ON DELETE CASCADE to ON DELETE SET NULL. Greenfield gets the migration automatically; existing v0.1.0 deployments run bin/rails generate typed_eav:install to copy and apply it. :destroy is the default and reproduces v0.1.0 behavior exactly. :nullify relies on the existing read-path orphan guard (v.field nil → skip), already documented in CONCERNS.md as intentional fail-soft. :restrict_with_error implemented via before_destroy that queries values.exists? and, if true, calls errors.add(:base, …) + throw(:abort) — mirrors AR's dependent: :restrict_with_error.

answered.question: Callback registration shape — how do user `on_value_change` and Phase 04/07 internal hooks coexist?
answered.answer: Public single proc per slot (Config.on_value_change, Config.on_field_change) backed by ActiveSupport::Configurable, plus a private internal-subscribers array that fires FIRST. User proc fires LAST. Phase 4 versioning + Phase 7 index DDL register on the internal list via a private register_internal_value_change / register_internal_field_change API. Matches ROADMAP literally; reassigning Config.on_value_change cannot silently disable Phase 4/7 features. Rejected: ActiveSupport::Notifications (changes ROADMAP-locked public shape, instrument Hash payloads don't fit positional (value, change_type, context) signature) and pure single proc with no internal list (forces users to chain Phase 4 versioning into their own proc).

answered.question: `:update` firing semantics — fire on every after_commit or only when typed value column changed?
answered.answer: Filter to saved_change_to_attribute?(field.class.value_column). Only fire :update when the typed column for the field's value_column actually changed. apply_pending_value (after_initialize) does not dirty the row, so this filter composes with the existing lifecycle. Avoids no-op version rows in Phase 4 and spurious matview refreshes in Phase 7. :create fires after first commit regardless; :destroy fires after destroy commit regardless. entity_id repointing is out-of-spec for Value and not fired.

answered.question: Error policy when a hook raises — pass-through, always-rescue, or split policy?
answered.answer: Split policy. Internal subscribers (Phase 4 versioning, Phase 7 index DDL regen) propagate exceptions — no rescue, failure is loud. User proc (Config.on_value_change, Config.on_field_change) wrapped in rescue StandardError; exception logged via Rails.logger.error with hook name + value/field id + change_type; does NOT propagate. Mirrors the codebase's fail-closed-defaults pattern: integrity feature loud, observability glue safe. No fallback Config.on_callback_error hook in this phase — deferred.

answered.question: `on_field_change` change_type set — does :rename exist as a distinct symbol, and when does it fire?
answered.answer: [:create, :update, :destroy, :rename]. :rename fires whenever `name` is among Field#saved_changes, even when name changed alongside other attrs (options, default_value_meta, sort_order, field_dependent) in the same save. Branching: created? → :create; destroyed? → :destroy; else if saved_change_to_attribute?(:name) → :rename; else :update. Rationale: Phase 7 needs DDL distinguishability — :rename triggers ALTER TABLE / matview regen, :update does not. False-positive cost is a redundant async DDL regen; false-negative cost is a corrupted matview where SQL column name doesn't match field name. Pick the safer side.

answered.question: Versioning opt-in granularity — gem-global flag, per-entity, or per-Field?
answered.answer: Master switch + per-entity. TypedEAV.config.versioning is a kill-switch (default false → Phase 4 internal subscriber not registered at engine boot, zero overhead). When true, the subscriber registers but only writes a version row when value.entity_type belongs to a host that opted in (`has_typed_eav versioned: true` or `include TypedEAV::Versioned`, recorded on the existing Registry entry). Decouples the master switch from the per-host decision. Rejected: gem-global on/off only (forces all-or-nothing — Phase 5 will introduce field types apps may want versioned only on specific hosts). Rejected: per-Field column on typed_eav_fields (granularity most apps don't need; requires migration for everyone).

answered.question: Version row jsonb shape for before_value / after_value — column-named, scalar wrapper, or full-row snapshot?
answered.answer: Column-named jsonb: {"<column_name>": <casted value>}. One key for current 17 single-cell field types; two keys for Phase 5 Currency ({"decimal_value": 99.99, "string_value": "USD"}). Subscriber reads field.class.value_columns (a list, defaulting to [value_column] for single-cell types). Forward-compatible with Phase 5 Currency at zero schema cost; queryable via jsonb operators. Empty {} means "no recorded value"; {"<column>": null} means "recorded NULL". Rejected: flat scalar {"v": <value>} (Currency forces a one-off branch and loses native column-key queryability). Rejected: full-row snapshot of all 7 typed columns (~6 redundant nulls per row, storage waste at scale).

answered.question: Value#revert_to(version) semantics — fires hooks (append-only) or raw write?
answered.answer: Append-only, fires hooks. revert_to writes the targeted version's before_value back via self.value = … (or per-column self[col] = … for multi-cell types like Currency) and save!. The existing after_commit :_dispatch_value_change_update chain fires; EventDispatcher routes through Versioning's internal subscriber (slot 0); a NEW version row is written where after_value matches the targeted version's before_value. User Config.on_value_change also fires — revert is a normal :update. Matches PaperTrail / Audited conventions. Caller can use with_context(reverted_from_version_id: v.id) { value.revert_to(v) } to record intent on the new version row; Versioning does NOT inject this automatically. Rejected: silent revert via update_columns (skips callbacks → no version row → audit gap). Rejected: hybrid Versioning.silent { value.revert_to(v) } API (parallel execution path, complexity for marginal gain).

answered.question: Config.actor_resolver returning nil — allow nil changed_by, reject the write, or fall back to a sentinel?
answered.answer: Allow nil. typed_eav_value_versions.changed_by is nullable. System writes (migrations, jobs, console, any path where actor_resolver returns nil) get changed_by: nil — symmetric with how scope_resolver returning nil yields no scope. Apps that need 'every write must have an actor' enforce it in their own actor_resolver lambda (e.g., raise unless Current.user) or via a downstream validation on the version row. Reasoning vs scope: scope is fail-closed because tenant isolation is catastrophic when missing; actor is degraded audit (recoverable, sometimes legitimate — system writes are real). Forcing actor present is an app-policy choice, not a gem-policy choice; strict-mode would reject every console save, migration backfill, and job without with_context(actor: ...) — hostile defaults. Rejected: raise TypedEAV::ActorRequired. Rejected: configurable sentinel actor (Config.system_actor) — kicks the policy decision down the road.

answered.question: Bulk write failure isolation in Entity.bulk_set_typed_eav_values — single transaction (all-or-nothing), savepoints per record (partial success), or per-record transactions (no outer atomicity)?
answered.answer: Savepoints per record inside an outer transaction. ActiveRecord::Base.transaction(requires_new: true) per record gives partial success matching the result shape (successes / errors_by_record): bad records roll back to their savepoint and surface in errors_by_record; good records commit on outer COMMIT. Outer-level failures (deadlock, connection drop) still abort the whole batch. Default on_error: :continue semantics implied by partial success; no explicit on_error: keyword in v0.6.0. Rejected: strict all-or-nothing (makes errors_by_record pointless), per-record transactions (loses cross-record atomicity, breaks Phase 3 on_value_change semantics).

answered.question: Field.import_schema conflict policy when (name, entity_type, scope, parent_scope) already exists with different attributes — silent skip, always overwrite, always error, or per-call flag?
answered.answer: on_conflict: keyword argument with values :error (default), :skip, :overwrite. Type changes (incompatible STI subclass swap, e.g., Field::String → Field::Decimal) ALWAYS raise regardless of flag, since the gem cannot infer a safe migration of existing typed values across *_value columns. Idempotence key remains (name, entity_type, scope, parent_scope); identical attributes are no-ops under any flag. Default :error surfaces drift loudly — a stale CI hash never silently reverts production. :skip covers 'ensure schema present' deploys. :overwrite covers admin force-sync. Mirrors Rails upsert_all on_duplicate: idiom. Rejected: silent-skip-only (drift goes unnoticed), always-overwrite (stale hash imports too dangerous as default), always-error (noisy for CI 'ensure present' use case).

answered.question: TypedEAV::CSVMapper.row_to_attributes — mapping shape, per-row return value, and streaming model?
answered.answer: Bundled decision: (1) Mapping shape — single hash. String keys map CSV headers ({"Email Address" => :email}); integer keys map column indexes for headerless files ({0 => :email}). No transform overrides in v0.6.0; callers preprocess CSV::Row before calling row_to_attributes. (2) Per-row return — always returns TypedEAV::CSVMapper::Result with .attributes / .errors / .success?. Never raises on row-level errors. Type coercion goes through existing Field#cast; cast failures land in .errors, not exceptions. Symmetric with bulk_set_typed_eav_values successes/errors_by_record so users learn one mental model. (3) Streaming — per-row API, no file-level orchestration in the library. Callers use CSV.foreach(path, headers: true) { |row| ... } to keep memory O(1). Rejected: raise-on-error per row (loses structural 'never fail-whole-import' promise), transform overrides in mapping (over-scoped for v0.6.0), library-side file orchestration (caller-orchestrated streaming covers the use case).

answered.question: version_grouping: option in bulk_set_typed_eav_values — what mechanism, what default mode, and what happens when Config.versioning = false?
answered.answer: Mechanism + default: add nullable, indexed version_group_id uuid column to typed_eav_value_versions (additive migration on top of Phase 4's table; uses disable_ddl_transaction! + algorithm: :concurrently per PATTERNS.md production-safety pattern). Bulk operations populate version_group_id; non-bulk writes leave it NULL (backward-compatible). Default version_grouping: :per_record when versioning is enabled — one uuid per record touched; all field changes for record X share that uuid. Aligns with savepoint-per-record boundary from bulk write failure isolation decision. :per_field also supported (one uuid per field touched across the bulk set) for 'what entities had field Y bulk-updated together?' queries. Disabled-versioning behavior: raise ArgumentError when version_grouping: is explicitly passed but Config.versioning = false. Omitting the option works in either env so env-conditional callers don't branch. Loud failure prevents silent no-op footgun. Rejected: collapse multiple field changes into single version row with NULL field_id (reshapes Phase 4 schema), use shared changed_at timestamp instead of column (ambiguous under concurrent bulks / clock skew), silently ignore when versioning disabled (silent no-op footgun), warn-and-continue (Rails moving away from this pattern).

inferred.text: Resolver protocol shape change is a documented breaking change for the v0.2 release; CHANGELOG and README must include migration notes for custom Config.scope_resolver implementations.
spec/regressions/review_round_2_scope_leak_spec.rb and review_round_3_collision_spec.rb gain parallel coverage for the parent_scope axis (atomic-bypass semantic preserved).

Field::Base and Section gain in-house partition-aware list helpers (move_higher / move_lower / move_to_top / move_to_bottom / insert_at) keyed by (entity_type, scope, parent_scope), with with_lock-based race safety and sort_order normalization to consecutive integers within partition. Boundary moves are no-ops. No new runtime dependency.
TypedEAV::Value defines a private UNSET_VALUE sentinel. typed_values.create(field: f) populates from field.default_value when value is unset; explicit nil short-circuits default population.

Field#backfill_default! iterates target entities via find_each(batch_size: 1000) with per-batch transactions, synchronous by default, idempotent on rerun, partition-aware via Phase 1's for_entity helper. Skip rule operates on 'non-nil typed column,' not 'Value row exists.'

New migration adds field_dependent string column on typed_eav_fields (null: false, default: 'destroy'), makes typed_eav_values.field_id nullable, and changes the FK from ON DELETE CASCADE to ON DELETE SET NULL. :destroy / :nullify / :restrict_with_error are the three allowed values, validated on Field::Base. :restrict_with_error mirrors AR semantics via before_destroy + errors.add(:base, ...) + throw(:abort).

Phase 02 ROADMAP goal language ('without adding new columns') is refined to 'additive schema changes only, defaults preserve v0.1.0 behavior, API surface remains backwards-compatible' to accommodate the field_dependent column and FK semantic change.

Config.on_value_change and Config.on_field_change are public single-proc slots backed by ActiveSupport::Configurable. A private TypedEAV::EventDispatcher (or equivalent) module exposes register_internal_value_change / register_internal_field_change for first-party features (Phase 4 versioning, Phase 7 index DDL regen). Internal subscribers fire FIRST in registration order; user proc fires LAST. Reassigning Config.on_value_change cannot disable internal subscribers.

Value `after_commit` dispatcher fires on_value_change with change_type :create on first commit, :destroy on destroy commit, and :update only when saved_change_to_attribute?(field.class.value_column) is true. No-op updates (no value-column change in saved_changes) do not fire :update.

Internal subscribers propagate exceptions raised inside their callable. User proc (Config.on_value_change, Config.on_field_change) is wrapped in rescue StandardError; raised exceptions are logged via Rails.logger.error with hook name + record id + change_type and do NOT propagate. No fallback Config.on_callback_error hook in this phase.

Field `after_commit` dispatcher fires on_field_change with change_type from the set [:create, :update, :destroy, :rename]. Branch: created? → :create; destroyed? → :destroy; else saved_change_to_attribute?(:name) → :rename (even when other attrs also changed in the same save); else → :update. Phase 7 matview DDL regen subscribes to :rename specifically.

TypedEAV.with_context(**kwargs) { } pushes a frozen pre-merged Hash onto a thread-local stack (`merged = stack.last&.merge(new) || new`); pops in `ensure`. Reading current context returns stack.last || {}. Shallow per-key merge across nesting; deep-merge of nested Hash values is NOT promised. Default empty context is {}. Mirrors with_scope's ensure-pop exception safety.

TypedEAV.config.versioning is a master kill-switch (default false). When false, no Phase 4 internal subscriber is registered with EventDispatcher.register_internal_value_change at engine boot — zero overhead for non-versioning apps. When true, the subscriber registers but only writes a version row when value.entity_type belongs to a host model that opted in via has_typed_eav versioned: true (or include TypedEAV::Versioned). Per-host opt-in is recorded on the existing Registry entry; non-opted entity types pay a Registry lookup per write and nothing more.

typed_eav_value_versions.before_value and .after_value are jsonb hashes keyed by typed-column name: {"<column_name>": <casted value>}. Versioning subscriber snapshots only the columns returned by field.class.value_columns (a list; defaults to [value_column] for single-cell types; Phase 5 Currency overrides to [:decimal_value, :string_value]). Empty {} means 'no recorded value'; {"<column>": null} means 'recorded NULL'. No full-row snapshot, no scalar wrapping.

Value#revert_to(version) is append-only and fires hooks. It writes the targeted version's before_value columns back via self.value = … (or per-column self[col] = … for multi-cell types) and save!. The existing Value after_commit :_dispatch_value_change_update chain fires; a new version row is written; Config.on_value_change user proc also fires. revert_to does NOT auto-inject a reverted_from_version_id field on the new version row's context — callers wrap the call in TypedEAV.with_context(reverted_from_version_id: v.id) { ... } if they want that recorded.

Config.actor_resolver mirrors Config.scope_resolver shape but with permissive nil semantics: a nil return is allowed and yields a version row with changed_by: nil. typed_eav_value_versions.changed_by is nullable. There is no TypedEAV::ActorRequired exception, no Config.system_actor sentinel fallback, and no Config.require_actor strict-mode in Phase 4. Apps that need stricter policy enforce it in their own actor_resolver lambda or via a downstream validation on the version row.
bulk_set_typed_eav_values uses savepoints per record inside an outer ActiveRecord transaction; result is { successes:, errors_by_record: }
Field.import_schema accepts on_conflict: keyword (:error default, :skip, :overwrite); incompatible field-type changes always raise

TypedEAV::CSVMapper.row_to_attributes returns TypedEAV::CSVMapper::Result (.attributes, .errors, .success?) and never raises on row-level errors; mapping is a hash with string keys (headers) or integer keys (indexes)

typed_eav_value_versions gets a nullable indexed version_group_id uuid column; bulk_set_typed_eav_values populates it with version_grouping: :per_record (default) or :per_field; explicit version_grouping: with versioning disabled raises ArgumentError

deferred.idea: Extract a shared Scopable concern between Section and Field once a third caller appears or implementations diverge.

Auto-coerce non-array returns from custom resolvers as [value, nil] (BC-shim alternative). Explicitly rejected in favor of the hard-break path; documented to prevent future reintroduction without a deliberate reversal.

Active Job auto-dispatch for Field#backfill_default! (defined?(::ActiveJob) soft-detect). Rejected for Phase 02 — adds complexity for an explicitly-invoked method; users can wrap in their own job. Revisit if multiple consumers report needing async backfill out of the box.
acts_as_list as a soft-detected ordering backend (use it if defined, fall back to in-house). Rejected as double-maintenance with no real win. Do not reintroduce without a deliberate reversal.

Configurable per-app default for field_dependent (e.g., Config.default_field_dependent = :nullify). Out of scope for Phase 02 — schema-level default 'destroy' is enforced. Add later only if a real consumer asks.
Cascade behavior on Section destroy. Phase 02 covers Field destroy only; Section cascade may surface in a later phase.

Config.use_active_support_notifications = true opt-in to also broadcast hook events via ActiveSupport::Notifications alongside the proc API. Revisit if multiple consumers want pub/sub semantics for observability tooling (Datadog, OpenTelemetry, etc.). Out of scope for Phase 03 — proc API is the canonical surface.

Deep-merge semantics for nested with_context (Hash#deep_merge). Rejected in favor of shallow merge for predictability. Revisit if a real use case for hierarchical context (e.g., nested operation tags) emerges.

Config.on_callback_error = ->(err, hook_name, record) { ... } for structured error routing (Sentry, custom logger, etc.). Phase 3 uses Rails.logger.error directly; defer until a consumer reports needing structured routing.

Config.value_change_filter opt-out to fire :update on every after_commit instead of only when the typed column changed. Out of scope for Phase 03 — the filter is a correctness invariant for Phase 4 versioning + Phase 7 matview, not an observability preference.

Snapshot-storage extension pattern (alternative to event-log default for high-volume apps). Roadmap explicitly mentions it as documented but out of scope for this phase. Document the extension shape in README; do not implement.
Branching/merging across version chains. Explicitly out of scope per roadmap; do not reintroduce without a deliberate reversal.

Versioning.silent { … } API for opt-out callbacks during specific writes. Rejected for revert_to (would create a parallel execution path); could resurface for bulk-import scenarios in Phase 6. Revisit only if Phase 6 surfaces a real requirement.

Strict-mode actor resolution (Config.require_actor = true) raising TypedEAV::ActorRequired on nil. Out of scope for Phase 4 — apps enforce strictness in their own resolver. Revisit only if a regulated-industry consumer reports needing it built-in.
Config.system_actor sentinel fallback when actor_resolver returns nil. Rejected — kicks the policy decision down the road. Apps that need a non-nil default set it in their own resolver lambda.

Per-Field versioning toggle (typed_eav_fields.versioned:boolean). Rejected for Phase 4 — granularity most apps don't need; requires migration for everyone. Revisit only if a real consumer reports needing per-field selectivity within an opted-in entity.
jsonb GIN indexes on typed_eav_value_versions.before_value / after_value for content queries. Out of scope for Phase 4. Add later only if a real querying use case surfaces.

Auto-injection of reverted_from_version_id on the new version row's context during Value#revert_to. Phase 4 leaves this to the caller via TypedEAV.with_context(reverted_from_version_id: v.id) { ... }. Revisit if the manual pattern proves error-prone.
on_error: keyword on bulk_set_typed_eav_values (:continue / :raise / :rollback_all) — re-evaluate after v0.6.0 if real callers ask for it
Transform overrides in CSV mapping ({header => {field:, transform:}})
Library-side whole-file CSV orchestration helper (caller-orchestrated streaming covers the use case in v0.6.0)
Bulk delete API for typed values
Schema migration tooling for incompatible field-type changes — currently always raises in import_schema
